L1.area: Query parameter filtering and form field handling layer

L2.description: An integer conversion vulnerability exists where user-supplied numeric filter values in exponential format with extremely large exponents can cause a denial of service when the value is converted to an integer, due to the absence of an upper bound validation on the input magnitude.

L3.area: Numeric query parameter filter and its form field validation logic
L3.description: An integer conversion vulnerability exists where user-supplied numeric filter values in exponential format with extremely large exponents (e.g., scientific notation) can cause a denial of service when the value is converted to an integer. The numeric filter's form field lacks an upper bound validator to limit the magnitude of input values before conversion.

ground_truth.reason: The NumberFilter class uses a DecimalField without a maximum value validator, allowing malicious input in exponential format with very large exponents to cause a denial of service during integer conversion.
